line: Implemented recursive file watching in `crates/saw-daemon/src/watcher.rs:1` using `notify::RecommendedWatcher`, with:
- guard-aware path normalization so out-of-scope writes feed existing `ScopeLeaking` classification
- graceful watcher error handling via warn logs
Also wired exports in `crates/saw-daemon/src/lib.rs:1` and added `notify = "6"` in `crates/saw-daemon/Cargo.toml:6`.
- Reused the existing `EventBus` + `AgentState::apply(AgentEvent::FileModified)` path instead of inventing a parallel guard-leak signal.

items: Reused the existing `EventBus` + `AgentState::apply(AgentEvent::FileModified)` path instead of inventing a parallel guard-leak signal.

line: - Kept guard violations as normal file events; the already-implemented classifier/state logic turns them into `AgentPhase::ScopeLeaking`.

items: Kept guard violations as normal file events; the already-implemented classifier/state logic turns them into `AgentPhase::ScopeLeaking`.

line: - Filtered out folder-only create/remove events and tracked file events only.

items: Filtered out folder-only create/remove events and tracked file events only.

line: - The repo already had `process_monitor.rs`; `lib.rs` needed to export watcher alongside it.

items: The repo already had `process_monitor.rs`; `lib.rs` needed to export watcher alongside it.

line: - Existing state/classifier code already supports watcher-driven scope leak detection, so watcher only needed to normalize and publish events correctly.

items: Existing state/classifier code already supports watcher-driven scope leak detection, so watcher only needed to normalize and publish events correctly.

line: - `watcher.rs` is implemented and tested, but not yet integrated into the CLI runtime loop in `src/cmd/watch.rs`; current work delivers the daemon-side watcher module requested.

items: `watcher.rs` is implemented and tested, but not yet integrated into the CLI runtime loop in `src/cmd/watch.rs`; current work delivers the daemon-side watcher module requested.

line: - `notify` backends can coalesce low-level FS events differently by platform, so tests assert semantic detection rather than exact raw notify sequences.

items: `notify` backends can coalesce low-level FS events differently by platform, so tests assert semantic detection rather than exact raw notify sequences.
